content.text: Referencing Files in The Files API
is the process of using the returned
in content blocks (document or image) within Messages API requests, allowing you to reuse the same uploaded file across multiple API calls without re-sending the file bytes.
instead of attaching the file content directly, enabling efficient document reuse and prompt caching integration.
Reference Multiple Files in One Request

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

# STEP 1: Upload file once
uploaded = client.beta.files.upload(
    file=(
        "financial_report.pdf",
        open("financial_report.pdf", "rb"),
        "application/pdf"
    )
)
file_id = uploaded.id
print(f"✓ Uploaded: {file_id}")

# STEP 2: Ask multiple questions using same file_id
queries = [
    "What are the revenue figures for Q1-Q4?",
    "What are the key expenses?",
    "Summarize the financial highlights",
    "What is the profit margin?"
]

# Each query uses the same file_id — no re-upload needed
for query in queries:
    response = client.beta.messages.create(
        model="claude-opus-4-8",
        max_tokens=16000,
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": [
                {"type": "text", "text": query},
                {
                    "type": "document",
                    "source": {
                        "type": "file",
                        "file_id": file_id  # ← Same file_id reused
                    }
                }
            ]
        }]
    )
    
    answer = next((b.text for b in response.content if b.type == "text"), "")
    print(f"\n{query}")
    print(f"→ {answer[:300]}...")

# STEP 3: Delete when done
client.beta.files.delete(file_id)
print(f"✓ Deleted: {file_id}")
"With regular file attachments, you send the file contents with every request, but with the Files API, you upload once and then reference the returned
Always include the beta header when referencing files:
- Official SDKs set this automatically when using
- Direct API calls require manual header inclusion
Send file bytes + input tokens (large payload)
- Upload, list, metadata retrieval, deletion, download =
from files attached in Messages requests

Scenario: 10 queries on same 100-page PDF

Traditional:
- 10 × 100-page PDF = 10 full file uploads
- Pay 10 × file bytes + input tokens

Files API:
- 1 × upload (FREE)
- 10 × input tokens only (cheaper)
"Type mismatches appear to be checked at the point of referencing via a
Pairs naturally with prompt caching for repeat use
API may change — use feedback form
Must explicitly delete to avoid storage costs
Files tied to workspace, not portable across orgs
Cannot download files you uploaded (only Claude-generated files)
